content:
# Sedimentary Geochemistry

**Summary**: The study of the chemical composition and distribution of elements in sedimentary rocks.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[1996-matter|Matter, 1996]] used sedimentary-geochemistry to assess the phosphorus content of the ore.

- [[2024-malik|Malik, 2024]] used sedimentary-geochemistry to interpret the Shuram excursion and marine influence.

- [[2025-alhubail|Alhubail, 2025]] used sedimentary-geochemistry to analyze the impact of CO2 on mudrock samples.

## Scientific Context

- [[1996-matter|Matter, 1996]] Geochemistry is essential for determining the economic grade of the ore.

- [[2024-malik|Malik, 2024]] used sedimentary-geochemistry to interpret the Shuram excursion and marine influence.

- [[2025-alhubail|Alhubail, 2025]] used sedimentary-geochemistry to analyze the impact of CO2 on mudrock samples.

## Measurement and Characterization Methods

- [[1996-matter|Matter, 1996]] Chemical analysis.

## Key Findings Across Sources

- Identified high-grade zones with up to 34% P2O5 in the Thaniyat Phosphorite Member. ([[1996-matter|Matter, 1996, p. 115]])


- δ13Ccarb values are still quite higher than the Shuram values, requiring explanation via depositional setting. ([[2024-malik|Malik, 2024, p. 102]])

- Chemical changes during carbonation are detectable through stable isotope shifts. ([[2025-alhubail|Alhubail, 2025, p. 35]])
